original: Cleveland's Chris Dudley misses 5 free throws during 1 foul attempt

Scientists discover how to convert normal cells into stem cells in mice

Irfan Pathan becomes first bowler to take a Test cricket hat-trick in the opening over of a match
Norwegian government proposes building a floating road tunnel as part of a new roadway between Kristiansand and Trondheim

6 Iranian-held US hostages escape with help of Canadians

International agreement to pay for and conserve 9th century Borobudur Temple in Indonesia, world's largest Buddhist temple (completed 1982). Beginnings of the World Heritage Convention.

American writerEdgar Allan Poe's poem "The Raven" 1st published in The Evening Mirror newspaper (New York City)

Leslie Bricusse, British stage and film composer and lyricist (Doctor Dolittle; Goodbye, Mr. Chips; "Goldfinger"; Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory - "The Candy Man"; "Pure Imagination"), born in Pinner, Middlesex, England (d. 2021)
